name: MDZ Advanced
description: Advanced conformance profile for MDZ documents requiring enterprise-grade features: JCS canonicalization, multi-signature chains, W3C DIDs, content-addressed storage, and full provenance DAGs. Not required for Core conformance. A document that validates against mdz-advanced-v1 also validates against mdz-core.

validation_rules.description: document.content_id must be present. This is the hash of the primary entry_point — enables verification that the rendered content matches what was signed.
document.content_id algorithm must be one of the v2.0 spec's accepted hash algorithms.
Every asset entry across all categories must have content_hash. Advanced profile does not accept legacy `checksum` field only — a migration path exists but must complete before Advanced validation.
security.signatures[] must have at least one entry. Documents without signatures are not Advanced-conformant.
Every signature entry must declare canonicalization: 'jcs' (RFC 8785). Without canonicalization, signature verification is ambiguous across implementations.
For every signatures[i] with i > 0, prev_signature must be set and equal to the hash of signatures[i-1].signature. Enforces chain consistency detectable via spec-level validator.
Every signature entry's signer must have a did field. Name-only signers are not Advanced-conformant — identity must be machine-resolvable.
Signer DIDs must use one of the registered DID methods on this allowlist. Invented methods (e.g., `did:ror`) are not registered and are rejected.
Signature algorithms restricted to modern, widely-implemented choices. RSA with <2048-bit keys or SHA-1 is rejected.
security.integrity.manifest_checksum must be present and valid. Without it, the manifest itself can be swapped under a valid signature.
If the document has a non-initial version, document.derived_from[] should reference the prior version. Enforces provenance DAG completeness.
If history.enabled is true AND versions.json contains >1 version, history.graph_file must be set and the file must be present.
Every asset referenced by content_hash should have a corresponding assets/by-hash/<algo>/<hex>.<ext> entry that is byte-identical. Enables content-first retrieval.
If content.locales is set, default must be in available[].tag. (Also enforced by MDZManifest.validate() in the reference impls.)
If interactivity.scripts[] contains any entry, integrity (SRI hash) must be set. Without SRI, the viewer cannot verify scripts haven't been swapped in transit.
security.permissions must be fully populated; Advanced profile does not accept implicit 'whatever the default is.'
Advanced profile rejects v1.1 deprecated fields: security.signature (singular), asset.checksum (use content_hash), etc. Accepting them would legitimize inconsistent manifests.

notes: Advanced profile is intentionally strict. Documents failing Advanced may still be valid against Core.
Advanced is appropriate for: archival deposits, legal documents, supply-chain-sensitive technical reports, high-stakes publications.
Most scientific papers will validate against scientific-paper-v1 (which is Core-based). Only papers that need full provenance chain + multi-sig should pursue Advanced.
Migrating from Core to Advanced: add DIDs to all signers, compute and add content_id and content_hashes, add JCS canonicalization to signatures. No breaking change to the file format; additive.
A document may declare BOTH a primary profile (e.g., scientific-paper-v1) and mdz-advanced-v1 in document.profiles[] — they validate independently and both must pass.
